audit_scope: 9 representative pages scanned (see scan_findings below): homepage + 1 each of page/post/retreat/ytt/recipe/accommodation/guest_yoga_teacher + 1 thank-you page

meta_pixel.notes: Fires fbq('init') + PageView on every content page
Second Meta pixel — confirm with marketing whether both are intentional. Running two pixels is unusual.

google_analytics_universal.reason: Universal Analytics shut down July 2023. Tag still in HTML but sends nothing.

google_analytics_4.reason: gtag config is called with empty id ('gtag("config","")') — unfinished setup

google_ads_conversion.notes: No AW-* conversion pixels on any page, including thank-you pages

crazy_egg.notes: Mentioned in original migration plan as a separate integration track. Not deployed on current site — confirm if still wanted on new site.

known_issues: Meta Pixel is NOT present on /opt-in-thank-you/ — form-complete events are not firing to Meta. Fix on new site: ensure pixel runs on all thank-you pages.
Dead UA tag should be removed — it loads code that does nothing, small perf hit.
REST-derived URL /guest_yoga_teacher/breathwork-retreat-with-cristiane-machado/ returns 404. Some URLs in url_inventory are stale — needs a pass to flag unreachable URLs.
todo: Confirm with marketing: are both Meta Pixels (331563397336091, 518225776084188) intentional?
Create a fresh GA4 property for anamaya.com and add measurement ID to new site
On new site, fire Meta Pixel PageView on every page INCLUDING thank-you pages
Decide: Crazy Egg yes or no
Add a URL-liveness pass to inventory (mark 404s in url_inventory)

scan_findings.label: facebook_conversions_api_script
scan_findings.notes: Matches <script src="connect.facebook.net/.../fbevents.js"> loader for Meta Pixel — same infrastructure, not a separate tool
